revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''二十五有'''（にじゅうご う）とは[[衆生]]が[[生死]]（しょうじ）流転する生存の世界を二十五種に分類されるあ…」
revision.text:
'''二十五有'''（にじゅうご う）とは[[衆生]]が[[生死]]（しょうじ）流転する生存の世界を二十五種に分類されるありよう。
その分類の内訳は、四洲（四有）、[[四悪趣]]（四有）、[[六欲天]]（六有）、[[梵天]]（一有）、無想天（一有）、五那含天（または五浄居天、ごじょうごてん）（一有）、四禅天（四有）、四空処天（四有）の二十五種からなる<ref name="koko-do">仏教辞典（浩々洞 編）p.998</ref><ref>Web版 新纂浄土宗大辞典/二十五有</ref>。
有（う）とは[[因果不亡]]、因によって必ず果を得るということを表している<ref name="koko-do" />。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/933394/505?viewMode=1 仏教辞典（浩々洞 編）]無我山房、大正3年発行 * [http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web版 新纂浄土宗大辞典/二十五有]
{{DEFAULTSORT:にしゆうこ う}} [[Category:仏教の宇宙観]] [[Category:仏教の名数25]]

'''二十五有'''（にじゅうご う）とは[[衆生]]が[[生死]]（しょうじ）流転する生存の世界を二十五種に分類されるありよう。
その分類の内訳は、四洲（四有）、[[四悪趣]]（四有）、[[六欲天]]（六有）、[[梵天]]（一有）、無想天（一有）、五那含天（または五浄居天、ごじょうごてん）（一有）、四禅天（四有）、四空処天（四有）の二十五種からなる<ref name="koko-do">仏教辞典（浩々洞 編）p.998</ref><ref>Web版 新纂浄土宗大辞典/二十五有</ref>。
有（う）とは「因果不亡」、因果は死なず、因によって必ず果を得るということを表している<ref name="koko-do" />。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/933394/505?viewMode=1 仏教辞典（浩々洞 編）]無我山房、大正3年発行 * [http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web版 新纂浄土宗大辞典/二十五有]
{{DEFAULTSORT:にしゆうこ う}} [[Category:仏教の宇宙観]] [[Category:仏教の名数25]]

revision.comment: Created page with "'''The Twenty-Five States of Existence''' (二十五有, ''Nijūgo U'') classify the realms of existence where [[sentient beings]] transmigrate through the cycles of [[birth and death]] (生死, ''shōji'') into 25 categories. The breakdown of these classifications consists of the following 25 types: the Four Continents (四洲, 四有), the [[Four Evil Realms]] (四悪趣, 四有), the [[Six Desire Heavens]] (六欲天, 六有), the [[Brahma Heaven]] (梵天, 一有),..."
revision.text:
'''The Twenty-Five States of Existence''' (二十五有, ''Nijūgo U'') classify the realms of existence where [[sentient beings]] transmigrate through the cycles of [[birth and death]] (生死, ''shōji'') into 25 categories.
The breakdown of these classifications consists of the following 25 types: the Four Continents (四洲, 四有), the [[Four Evil Realms]] (四悪趣, 四有), the [[Six Desire Heavens]] (六欲天, 六有), the [[Brahma Heaven]] (梵天, 一有), the Heaven of Non-Perception (無想天, 一有), the Five Pure Abodes (五那含天, ''or'' 五浄居天, ''Gojōgoten'', 一有), the Four Form Realms (四禅天, 四有), and the Four Formless Realms (四空処天, 四有).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (Edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Jōdo Sect Buddhist Encyclopedia - Twenty-Five States of Existence]</ref>
The term "existence" (有, ''u'') signifies "the non-perishing of cause and effect," meaning that causes inevitably lead to effects.<ref name="koko-do" />
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/933394/505?viewMode=1 Buddhist Dictionary (Edited by Kōkōdō)] Mugazanbō, published in Taishō 3 * [http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Jōdo Sect Buddhist Encyclopedia - Twenty-Five States of Existence]
[[Category:Buddhist Cosmology]] [[Category:Buddhist Numerical Categories]] [[ja:二十五有]]

'''The Twenty-Five States of Existence''' (二十五有, ''Nijūgo U'') classify the realms of existence where [[sentient beings]] transmigrate through the cycles of [[birth and death]] (生死, ''shōji'') into 25 categories.
The breakdown of these classifications consists of the following 25 types: the Four Continents (四洲, 四有), the [[Four Evil Realms]] (四悪趣, 四有), the [[Six Desire Heavens]] (六欲天, 六有), the [[Brahma Heaven]] (梵天, 一有), the Heaven of Non-Perception (無想天, 一有), the Five Pure Abodes (五那含天, ''or'' 五浄居天, ''Gojōgoten'', 一有), the Four Form Realms (四禅天, 四有), and the Four Formless Realms (四空処天, 四有).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (Edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Jōdo Sect Buddhist Encyclopedia - Twenty-Five States of Existence]</ref>
The term "existence" (有, ''u'') signifies "the non-perishing of cause and effect," meaning that causes inevitably lead to effects.<ref name="koko-do" />
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/933394/505?viewMode=1 Buddhist Dictionary (Edited by Kōkōdō)] Mugazanbō, published in Taishō 3 * [http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Jōdo Sect Buddhist Encyclopedia - Twenty-Five States of Existence]

'''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the {{ill|sentient beings|ja|衆生}} and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.
The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣}} (shiakushu), {{ill|Six Desire Heavens|ja|六欲天}} (roku yokuten), {{ill|Brahma Heavens|ja|梵天}} (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />

[[Category:Buddhist Cosmology]] [[Category:Buddhist Numerical Categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:二十五有]]

'''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the {{ill|sentient beings|ja|衆生}} and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.
The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣}} (shiakushu), {{ill|Six Desire Heavens|ja|六欲天}} (roku yokuten), {{ill|Brahma Heavens|ja|梵天}} (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />

The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣}} (shiakushu), {{ill|Six Desire Heavens|ja|六欲天}} (roku yokuten), {{ill|Brahma|lt=Brahma Heavens|ja|梵天}} (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/933394/505?viewMode=1 Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō)] Mugasanbō, published in Taishō 3 (1914). * [http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]

'''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the {{ill|sentient beings|ja|衆生}} and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.
The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣}} (shiakushu), {{ill|Six Desire Heavens|ja|六欲天}} (roku yokuten), {{ill|Brahma|lt=Brahma Heavens|ja|梵天}} (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />

'''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the {{ill|sentient beings|ja|衆生}} and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.
The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣}} (shiakushu), {{ill|Six Desire Heavens|ja|六欲天}} (roku yokuten), {{ill|Brahma|lt=Brahma Heavens|ja|梵天}} (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />

'''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the {{ill|sentient beings|ja|衆生}} and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.
The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣}} (shiakushu), {{ill|Six Desire Heavens|ja|六欲天}} (roku yokuten), {{ill|Brahma|lt=Brahma Heavens|ja|梵天}} (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />

'''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the {{ill|sentient beings|ja|衆生}} and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.
The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣}} (shiakushu), {{ill|Six Desire Heavens|ja|六欲天}} (roku yokuten), {{ill|Brahma|lt=Brahma Heavens|ja|梵天}} (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />

'''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the {{ill|sentient beings|ja|衆生}} and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.
The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣}} (shiakushu), {{ill|Six Desire Heavens|ja|六欲天}} (roku yokuten), {{ill|Brahma|lt=Brahma Heavens|ja|梵天}} (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />
== References ==
{{Reflist}}

'''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the {{ill|sentient beings|ja|衆生}} and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.
The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣}} (shiakushu), {{ill|Six Desire Heavens|ja|六欲天}} (roku yokuten), {{ill|Brahma|lt=Brahma Heavens|ja|梵天}} (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />

'''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the {{ill|sentient beings|ja|衆生}} and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.
The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣}} (shiakushu), {{ill|Six Desire Heavens|ja|六欲天}} (roku yokuten), {{ill|Brahma|lt=Brahma Heavens|ja|梵天}} (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />

The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣}} (shiakushu), {{ill|Six Desire Heavens|ja|六欲天}} (roku yokuten), {{ill|Brahma|lt=Brahma Heavens|ja|梵天}} (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/933394/505?viewMode=1 Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō)] Mugasanbō, published in Taishō 3 (1914). * [http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]

revision.text:
'''二十五有'''（にじゅうご う）とは[[衆生]]が[[生死]]（しょうじ）流転する生存の世界を二十五種に分類されるありよう。
その分類の内訳は、四洲（四有）、[[四悪趣]]（四有）、[[六欲天]]（六有）、[[梵天]]（一有）、無想天（一有）、五那含天（または五浄居天、ごじょうごてん）（一有）、四禅天（四有）、四空処天（四有）の二十五種からなる<ref name="koko-do">仏教辞典（浩々洞 編）p.998</ref><ref>Web版 新纂浄土宗大辞典/二十五有</ref>。
有（う）とは「因果不亡」、因果は死なず、因によって必ず果を得るということを表している<ref name="koko-do" />。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/933394/505?viewMode=1 仏教辞典（浩々洞 編）]無我山房、大正3年発行 * [http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web版 新纂浄土宗大辞典/二十五有]
{{DEFAULTSORT:にしゆうこ う}} [[Category:仏教の宇宙観]] [[Category:仏教の名数25]]

[[ja:二十五有]]
[[sn:Twenty-Five States of Existence]] [[sn:wenty-Five States of Existence]]
<!--Twenty-Five States of Existence--><!--Twenty-Five States of Existence--><!--Twenty-Five States of Existence--> [[Category:仏教の名数25]] [[Category:Buddhist cosmology]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

'''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the [[:en:Sentient beings (Buddhism)|sentient beings]] and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cycle of birth and death|qq=}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.
The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Continents|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Evil Realms|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三悪趣}} (shiakushu), {{ill|Six Desire Heavens|ja|六欲天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Desire Heavens|qq=}} (roku yokuten), [[:en:Brahmā (Buddhism)|Brahma Heavens]] (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-perception Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Meditation Heavens|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Formless Heavens|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/933394/505?viewMode=1 Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō)] Mugasanbō, published in Taishō 3 (1914). * [http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]

'''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the [[:en:Sentient beings (Buddhism)|sentient beings]] and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cycle of birth and death|qq=}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.
The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Continents|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Evil Realms|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三悪趣}} (shiakushu), {{ill|Six Desire Heavens|ja|六欲天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Six Desire Heavens|qq=}} (roku yokuten), [[:en:Brahmā (Buddhism)|Brahma Heavens]] (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-perception Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Meditation Heavens|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Formless Heavens|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/933394/505?viewMode=1 Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō)] Mugasanbō, published in Taishō 3 (1914). * [http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]
{{translated page|ja|二十五有|version=100019796|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q97160792]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the [[:en:Sentient beings (Buddhism)|sentient beings]] and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cycle of birth and death|qq=|1=cycle of birth and death|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574577}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.
The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Continents|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Evil Realms|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三悪趣|1=Four Evil Realms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355419}} (shiakushu), {{ill|six heavens of the desire realm|ja|六欲天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/six heavens of the desire realm|qq=}} (roku yokuten), [[:en:Brahmā (Buddhism)|Brahma Heavens]] (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-perception Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Meditation Heavens|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Formless Heavens|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />

'''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the [[:en:Sentient beings (Buddhism)|sentient beings]] and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cycle of birth and death|qq=|1=cycle of birth and death|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574577}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.

The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Continents|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Evil Realms|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三悪趣|1=Four Evil Realms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355419}} (shiakushu), {{ill|six heavens of the desire realm|ja|六欲天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/six heavens of the desire realm|qq=}} (roku yokuten), [[:en:Brahmā (Buddhism)|Brahma Heavens]] (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-perception Heaven|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Meditation Heavens|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Formless Heavens|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>

Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the [[:en:Sentient beings (Buddhism)|sentient beings]] and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cycle of birth and death|1=cycle of birth and death|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574577}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.

The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Continents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Evil Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三悪趣|1=Four Evil Realms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355419}} (shiakushu), {{ill|six heavens of the desire realm|ja|六欲天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/six heavens of the desire realm|qq=}} (roku yokuten), [[:en:Brahmā (Buddhism)|Brahma Heavens]] (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-perception Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Meditation Heavens|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Formless Heavens|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>

The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:二十五有]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q97160792]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the [[:en:Sentient beings (Buddhism)|sentient beings]] and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cycle of birth and death|1=cycle of birth and death|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574577}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.
The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Continents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Evil Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三悪趣|1=Four Evil Realms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355419}} (shiakushu), {{ill|six heavens of the desire realm|ja|六欲天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/six heavens of the desire realm|qq=}} (roku yokuten), [[:en:Brahmā (Buddhism)|Brahma Heavens]] (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-perception Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Meditation Heavens|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Formless Heavens|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/933394/505?viewMode=1 Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō)] Mugasanbō, published in Taishō 3 (1914). * [http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]

The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Continents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Evil Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三悪趣|1=Four Evil Realms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355419}} (shiakushu), {{ill|six heavens of the desire realm|ja|六欲天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/six heavens of the desire realm|qq=}} (roku yokuten), [[:en:Brahmā (Buddhism)|Brahma Heavens]] (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-perception Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Meditation Heavens|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Formless Heavens|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:二十五有]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:二十五有]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:二十五有]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q97160792]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the [[:en:Sentient beings (Buddhism)|sentient beings]] and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cycle of birth and death|1=cycle of birth and death|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574577}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.
The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Continents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Evil Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三悪趣|1=Four Evil Realms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355419}} (shiakushu), {{ill|six heavens of the desire realm|ja|六欲天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/six heavens of the desire realm|qq=}} (roku yokuten), [[:en:Brahmā (Buddhism)|Brahma Heavens]] (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-perception Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Meditation Heavens|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Formless Heavens|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />

'''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the {{ill|Sentient beings (Buddhism)|en|Sentient beings (Buddhism)|WD=Q15276545|lt=sentient beings}} and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cycle of birth and death|1=cycle of birth and death|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574577}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.
The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Continents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Evil Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三悪趣|1=Four Evil Realms|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355419}} (shiakushu), {{ill|six heavens of the desire realm|ja|六欲天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/six heavens of the desire realm|qq=}} (roku yokuten), {{ill|Brahmā (Buddhism)|en|Brahmā (Buddhism)|WD=Q289162|lt=Brahma Heavens}} (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-perception Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Meditation Heavens|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Formless Heavens|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />

'''Nijūgoyū''' (二十五有) refers to the classification of the {{ill|Sentient beings (Buddhism)|en|Sentient beings (Buddhism)|WD=Q15276545|lt=sentient beings|1=Sentient beings (Buddhism)}} and their existence in the {{ill|cycle of birth and death|ja|生死|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cycle of birth and death|1=Q11574577|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574577}} (shōji) into twenty-five types.
The breakdown of this classification consists of twenty-five categories: the Four Continents ({{ill|Four Continents|ja|四洲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Continents|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shiyū), {{ill|Four Evil Realms|ja|四悪趣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Evil Realms|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 三悪趣|1=Q11355419|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11355419}} (shiakushu), {{ill|six heavens of the desire realm|ja|六欲天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/six heavens of the desire realm|qq=}} (roku yokuten), {{ill|Brahmā (Buddhism)|en|Brahmā (Buddhism)|WD=Q289162|lt=Brahma Heavens|1=Brahmā (Buddhism)}} (bonten, one type), Non-perception Heaven ({{ill|Non-perception Heaven|ja|無想天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Non-perception Heaven|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, musōten, one type), Five Pure Abodes ({{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五那含天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Five Pure Abodes|ja|五浄居天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Pure Abodes|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, gojōgoten, one type), Four Meditation Heavens ({{ill|Four Meditation Heavens|ja|四禅天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Meditation Heavens|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shizen-ten, four types), and Four Formless Heavens ({{ill|Four Formless Heavens|ja|四空処天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Four Formless Heavens|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, shikūshoten, four types).<ref name="koko-do">Buddhist Dictionary (edited by Kōkōdō), p.998</ref><ref>[http://jodoshuzensho.jp/daijiten/index.php/%E4%BA%8C%E5%8D%81%E4%BA%94%E6%9C%89 Web Edition: New Compilation Jōdo Sect Great Dictionary/Nijūgoyū]</ref>
The term "Yū" (有) signifies "the persistence of cause and effect," indicating that causes inevitably lead to results.<ref name="koko-do" />
